title: 5 Ergonomic Chairs Under ₱10,000 That Saved My Back
excerpt: Working from home in Cebu? These budget ergonomic chairs will fix your back pain without breaking the bank.
meta_description: Best budget ergonomic chairs in the Philippines for remote workers. Top picks under ₱10k with affiliate links.
content:
Sitting 8+ hours a day? Your chair matters.

Our top pick: Sihoo M57 Ergonomic Chair – Check price on Lazada (affiliate)
We earn a commission if you click – at no extra cost.

After testing 5 chairs under ₱10k, here are the winners...

1. Sihoo M57

Adjustable lumbar, mesh back, headrest. Best value for Filipino remote workers dealing with tropical heat.

2. Furnitura F08

Great for tall users. Wide seat and breathable mesh keep you cool during humid afternoons.

3. Hbada Ergonomic Office Chair

Compact design perfect for small condo home offices in Manila or Cebu.

Key Takeaway

Bottom Line

Invest in a chair with adjustable lumbar support and breathable mesh. Your back will thank you after those long Zoom meetings.

title: How to Set Up a Dual Monitor Without Clutter
excerpt: Cable management, monitor arms, and affordable accessories for a clean home office.
meta_description: Step-by-step guide to a clutter-free dual monitor setup. Best monitor arms and cable organizers for home offices.
content:
A dual monitor boosts productivity by 42% – but only if done right.

1. Use a monitor arm

Free up desk space instantly. A good monitor arm in the Philippines costs around ₱1,500 to ₱3,000 on Lazada or Shopee.

2. Hide your cables

Use adhesive cable clips and a cable raceway. Total cost: under ₱500.

Best budget monitor arm: North Bayou F80 – Check on Lazada (affiliate)
We earn a commission if you click – at no extra cost.

Then hide cables with adhesive clips from your local hardware store or Shopee.

Key Takeaway

Bottom Line

A monitor arm plus cable management under ₱2,000 transforms your desk. Cleaner space means clearer mind.
